_comment_campaign: Pins the scenario: William is calling the founder of a DIGITAL MARKETING
AGENCY to sell white-label AI products they can resell to their own
clients. Every capability in the campaign is real and present in the
corpus (chatbots, multi-tier reseller platform, multi-tenant SaaS,
agentic automation, client dashboards) — see llm/campaigns.py.
Without a campaign the agent re-improvises the call each run, which is
_comment_voice: Empty = use the registry default, which prefers ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 and
falls back to Deepgram automatically when ELEVENLABS_API_KEY is absent.
The real voices are discovered from the ElevenLabs account at worker
startup, so pinning an id here would rot. Pick one live in the demo's
voice picker instead — switching takes effect on the next sentence.
_comment_llm: openai/gpt-4o-mini on a PAID key. This is a rate-limit decision, not a
model-quality one. Measured against this agent's ~3,000-token turn:
gemini free  5 turns/min (hard 5 RPM cap, confirmed by a live 429)
groq free   ~4 turns/min (12k TPM budget / 3k per turn)
A demo conversation is 15-30 turns, so both free tiers run dry mid-call
and the agent goes silent — which is indistinguishable from a crash in
Gemini 2.5 Flash is the better model for persona nuance and gets prompt
caching; switch back to it if GEMINI_API_KEY ever moves to a paid tier.
_comment_temperature: 0.6, against 0.0 for the dispatch tenants. A dispatcher reading back a
rate should be deterministic; an SDR at 0.0 delivers every line with the
same cadence, and that sameness is what makes an agent sound synthetic —
more than the voice does. 0.6 buys rhythm variation.
This does NOT loosen grounding: facts are constrained by the retrieval
prompt and enforced by the pre-TTS verifier, which blocks any number the
agent did not retrieve. Temperature moves phrasing, not truth.
Above ~0.8 it starts embellishing past the sources. Do not raise it.
